title: [Question] Is systemd-localed.service supposed to work inside a podman container?
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Question: is systemd-localed meant to work inside a container or is this a natural restriction?

I am getting
```
Jan 29 23:34:35 7f0a49aa8d77 systemd[142]: systemd-localed.service: Failed to set up network namespacing: Operation not permitted                                      
Jan 29 23:34:35 7f0a49aa8d77 systemd[142]: systemd-localed.service: Failed at step NETWORK spawning /usr/lib/systemd/systemd-localed: Operation not permitted          
```
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.
```
[root@7f0a49aa8d77 /]# localectl
Could not get properties: Connection timed out

Jan 29 23:34:35 7f0a49aa8d77 dbus-daemon[29]: [system] Activating via systemd: service name='org.freedesktop.locale1' unit='dbus-org.freedesktop.locale1.service' requested by ':1.1' (uid=0 pid=141 comm="localectl " label="system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c306,c310")                                                                     
Jan 29 23:34:35 7f0a49aa8d77 systemd[1]: Starting Locale Service...
Jan 29 23:34:35 7f0a49aa8d77 systemd[142]: systemd-localed.service: Failed to set up network namespacing: Operation not permitted                                      
Jan 29 23:34:35 7f0a49aa8d77 systemd[142]: systemd-localed.service: Failed at step NETWORK spawning /usr/lib/systemd/systemd-localed: Operation not permitted          
Jan 29 23:34:35 7f0a49aa8d77 systemd[1]: systemd-localed.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=225/NETWORK                                                 
Jan 29 23:34:35 7f0a49aa8d77 systemd[1]: systemd-localed.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.                                                                      
Jan 29 23:34:35 7f0a49aa8d77 systemd[1]: Failed to start Locale Service.
Jan 29 23:35:00 7f0a49aa8d77 dbus-daemon[29]: [system] Failed to activate service 'org.freedesktop.locale1': timed out (service_start_timeout=25000ms)
```

2.

3.

**Describe the results you received:**
```
[root@7f0a49aa8d77 /]# localectl
Could not get properties: Connection timed out
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
```
$ localectl
   System Locale: LANG=en_US.UTF-8
       VC Keymap: us
      X11 Layout: us
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Tries to start systemd-localed by dbus activation but fails with:

```
systemd-localed.service: Failed to set up network namespacing: Operation not permitted             
```


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.7.0-2.fc31.x86_64
# running a centos:8 systemd container on Fedora 31 host with cgroupsv2
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
Yikes - just observed that the default systemd-localed  unit file uses `Private*` settings so it's not going to work inside podman. Sorry for the noise.

```
[Service]
PrivateTmp=yes
PrivateDevices=yes
PrivateNetwork=yes
```
